evidence: The word 'exterior' strongly resonates with **scale.cosmic** (universal scope), **ontological.material** (physical nature), and **experience.discovery** (exploring the unknown). It also aligns with **resonance.essential** (touching core existence) and **interconnection.resonant** (deep connections). Lower scores reflect its focus on external physical reality rather than abstract or relational dynamics.

The word 'external' strongly resonates with cosmic scale (5), material ontology (7), and existential resonance (4). It emphasizes permanence (4) and necessary existence (5), aligning with the external world's enduring, transcendent nature beyond immediate experience.

The highest scores reflect the externality's enduring existence (permanence), its embodiment in ecosystems and material reality, and its sensory/aesthetic resonance. Perception and experience are central to engaging with the external world, while its essential nature touches core existence beyond self.

Extraneous resonates most with elements outside one's immediate sphere (scale.individual, ontological.material/relational). It is perceived as external (epistemological.perception/identification) and exists independently (interconnection.independent). Superficial connections to structure (resonance.superficial) and ethical considerations (value_generation.ethical) also align with its meaning.

Extraneousness strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (5) because it involves judgments of meaning and relevance. It scores moderately in scale.individual (2), reflecting its relation to personal perspective, and epistemological.identification/experience (2 each), as it involves categorizing or experiencing elements as outside one's sphere. Resonance.superficial (3) and lawfulness.contingent (2) reflect its surface-level nature and dependence on context. Other scores are weak or zero due to lack of direct connection.

The word 'extrinsic' strongly resonates with scales (community to cosmic), material ontology, perception, and structural/essential resonance. It ties to external patterns (structural), core essence (essential), and practical utility (functional). Lower scores reflect its focus on external existence without emphasizing time, creation, or moral dimensions.

The word 'inside' strongly resonates with the individual scale (10) and ontological semantic layer (7), as it represents the core of one's personal sphere contrasting the outer realm. Resonance.essential (8) and symmetry.inclusive (6) highlight its role as an essential, self-contained value. Lower scores reflect its lack of direct temporal or cosmic relevance to the external world.

The word 'open' strongly resonates with the external realm's vastness and accessibility (scale.cosmic=3, ontological.material=3). It aligns with direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception=4) and embodied experience (epistemological.experience=4) of the external world. The essential resonance (resonance.essential=3) and transformative potential (symmetry.transformative=3) reflect openness as a core feature of the external world's structure. The aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic=4) captures the beauty of unbounded spaces, while discovery (experience.discovery=4) highlights exploration beyond one's immediate sphere.

The word 'open' as the external realm strongly resonates with cosmic scale (9), material ontology (10), and essential/existential resonance (8-7), reflecting its vast, enduring nature and deep connection to existence. Discovery (9) and perception (6) highlight exploration of the unknown, while functional/aesthetic value (7-6) underscores its practical and sensory aspects.

Open air strongly resonates with perception (direct sensory experience) and material ontology (physical presence). Its aesthetic value and predictability reflect its role in the external world. Essential resonance captures its foundational role in the external realm, while ecosystem and cosmic scales highlight its universal presence.

The word 'out' strongly resonates with ontological.material (7) as it denotes the physical external world. Scale.cosmic (7) reflects its universal scope. Experience.discovery (7) captures encountering the unknown beyond one's sphere. Lower scores align with perception/experience (5/5) of the external and functional utility (5) in engaging with it.

Out-of-doors strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and ecosystemic scale (scale.ecosystem). Its permanence and continuity reflect its enduring presence beyond human control. Aesthetic value and cyclical patterns (seasons) further align with its essence.

The outdoors is fundamentally physical (ontological.material 10) and persists over time (temporal.continuation 7). Its sensory immediacy (epistemological.perception 9) and lived experience (epistemological.experience 8) align with its role as an independent, cyclical (symmetry.cyclical 6) system. Aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic 9) and functional utility (value_generation.functional 6) reflect its dual role in beauty and practicality.

Outdoorsy strongly resonates with the physical world (ontological.material), direct sensory experience in nature (epistemological.experience), and aesthetic appreciation of the outdoors (value.aesthetic). It connects deeply with ecosystems (scale.ecosystem) and reflects a core essence tied to nature (resonance.essential).

The word 'outer' strongly resonates with semantic meaning (7) as it defines the essence of being external. Its essential core (7) and structural correspondence (5) reflect the foundational contrast between inner and outer realms. The cosmic scale (7) and independent existence (5) emphasize its vast, self-sustaining nature. Discovery (6) captures the encounter with unknown external domains.

The word 'outermost' strongly resonates with scale.cosmic (5) as it denotes the furthest extent of existence. Ontological layers like relational (5), structural (4), and semantic (5) reflect its role in connections, organizational patterns, and meaning. Interconnection.independent (5) and resonant (5) highlight its self-contained yet deeply connected nature. Resonance.essential (5) underscores its core essence, while experience.discovery (5) emphasizes exploration of the unknown. Value.sacred (5) captures its transcendent significance beyond ordinary realms.

The word 'outmost' strongly resonates with the outermost extent of existence (scale.cosmic:10), emphasizing boundaries and transcendence (resonance.existential:6). It relates to discovery beyond the immediate (experience.discovery:8) and touches on existential and semantic layers (ontological.semantic:5, resonance.essential:5).

The word 'outside' strongly resonates with the external world's material existence (ontological.material), its sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and its essential boundary with the self (resonance.essential). It also reflects the external world's persistence (temporal.permanence) and its aesthetic qualities (value_generation.aesthetic).

The word 'outside' strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material) and sensory perception (epistemological.perception). Its essential and existential resonance (resonance.essential/existential) reflects its core role as the external realm beyond self. Interconnection.resonant and scale.ecosystem highlight its interconnected, systemic nature. Lower scores reflect its neutrality regarding creation, ethics, or cosmic scales.

The term 'satellite' strongly resonates with stellar scale (10), material existence (10), and functional utility (8). Its relational connections (8) and predictable orbital dynamics (lawfulness.predictable: 10) align with the external realm's systemic nature. Aesthetic and sacred undertones (3/2) reflect cultural or observational significance.
